steps.title: Step 1 — Identify the rule
steps.content: Verb tense. The time adverb "Today" at the start of the second sentence signals present time, so the verb that fills the blank must be in the simple present.

steps.title: Step 2 — Apply the rule
steps.content: "Today, Paik is considered the first video artist" correctly pairs the present-time adverb "Today" with the present-tense linking verb "is" in this passive construction.

steps.title: Step 3 — Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'will be': verb tense error — future tense conflicts with the present-time adverb "Today." ✗
  • Choice B — 'had been': verb tense error — past perfect requires a later past reference point, which the sentence lacks. ✗
  • Choice C — 'was': verb tense error — simple past contradicts "Today," which names a present condition. ✗
That matches choice D — D is correct.
